Attributes.Value: Program content and objectives must be clearly outlined and well defined. Program content should reflect specified philosophy and objectives and be organized to promote integrated learning opportunities whilst stressing the link between theory and practice.

Program review must be an active, dynamic and ongoing process that addresses both quality and effectiveness in the preparation of early childhood education graduates, and quality of service in the field.
The program must provide appropriate and sufficient space, equipment and resources, and be accessible and responsive to the needs of a variety of learners.
The program must provide appropriate and sufficient space, equipment and resources, and is accessible and responsive to the needs of a variety of learners.

The program must be well managed, ensure accountability, promote confidence and trust in the program for students, staff, the public and the Registry, ensuring that students meet the required competencies for certification. If there is no specific ECE policy, provide the departmental or institutional policy that the ECE department is required to follow.

The program must be well managed, ensure accountability, promote confidence and trust in the program for students, staff, the public and the Registry, ensuring that students meet the required competencies for certification. If there is no specific ECE policy, provide the departmental or institutional policy that the ECE department is required to follow.

Students must participate in field-based child care facility experience under the supervision and guidance of both a certified ECE (practicum mentor) on site, and a post-secondary practicum instructor in B.C. It is the expectation of the Registry that the minimum requirements for practicum, as stated in the Linking Competencies document, be maintained and that practicum placement be completed in B.C. Practicums should be woven throughout the program to provide frequent opportunities to meaningfully integrate theory to practice and reflect. Observation by practicum instructors is required and student supervision (virtually and/or in person) cannot rely solely on reports of a practicum mentor.

Students must participate in field-based child care facility experience under the supervision and guidance of both a certified ECE (practicum mentor) and a post-secondary practicum instructor in B.C. It is the expectation of the Registry that the minimum requirements for practicum, as stated in the Linking Competencies document, be maintained and that practicum placement be completed in B.C. Observation by practicum instructors is required (virtually and/or in person) and student supervision cannot rely solely on reports of a practicum mentor.

In conjunction with the Community Care and Assisted Living Act and Child Child Care Licensing Regulation setting legislative requirements, the B.C. Child Care Sector Occupational Competencies (BCCCSOC) sets out the occupational standards for ECEs and ECE Assistants to be certified and to maintain certification in B.C. Recognized early childhood education programs are required to meet the standards established in the BCCCSOC. The Linking Competencies document complements the BCCCSOC by organizing the occupational standards into required minimum post-secondary areas of instruction and associated hours of instruction for basic and post-basic programs.

The program must be well managed, ensure accountability, promote confidence and trust in the program for students, staff, the public and the Registry, ensuring that students meet the required competencies for certification. If there is no specific ECE policy, provide the departmental or institutional policy that the ECE department is required to follow.

Personnel (for example, coordinators, instructors, practicum instructors, administrators, technical staff) must have the necessary qualifications, relevant experience and leadership abilities to provide early childhood education instruction to adult learners and to model professional behavior.

Admission criteria policies must be clear and easy to understand, providing prospective students the necessary information about program prerequisites and requirements, such as criminal record checks, fluency in English or entrance exams.

The program must provide appropriate equipment and resources, and be accessible and responsive to the needs of a variety of learners.
Appropriate and sufficient resources are available to those involved in online programming.

Instructional methods and strategies must reflect those contemporarily accepted in the post-secondary and early childhood education field and aim to support the diverse needs of adult learners through respect of diversity in the culture, languages, accessibility needs and learning styles of students.

Student evaluations must be continual and cumulative.

Recognizing the diverse needs of remote students, strong communication with the instructor is essential to learner success. Consideration must also be given to the balance between synchronous and asynchronous delivery, which can significantly impact learning experience.
